response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced, versatile software engineer (~15 years) with strong full-stack, testing, and distributed-systems credentials, but the role is specifically a C++ developer position requiring deep C++/STL, multi-threaded networked systems, message brokers, MQTT/REST IoT work, and IEC 62443 secure development. The resume shows only brief, dated C/C++ exposure (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project), with the bulk of recent work in Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang. There is no evidence of message-broker architectures, MQTT, industrial automation, or IEC 62443/SDL experience, making this a poor match for the core technical requirements.
strengths: Extensive multi-domain experience including high-throughput, low-latency distributed services and event-driven platforms
Strong software-craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, mentoring, workshops)
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in systems-level audio work
concerns: C/C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; no recent STL or modern C++ work
No evidence of MQTT, message brokers, IEC 62443/SDL, or industrial-automation/IoT experience
Recent stack centers on web/backend (Node, TypeScript, DLang) rather than the role's core competencies

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / MQTT experience
Broad senior engineering and distributed-systems experience
